title: request_bugfix: shopsystem-bc-launcher — propagate credentials into BC containers (gh, claude, gitconfig)
description: bc-container launch's docker run command (observed empirically 2026-05-22 attempting to launch shopsystem-docs) does NOT mount or propagate the credentials required for the launched BC to function. The lead-shop devcontainer.json mounts three credential paths that are essential to any in-container shop's operation: (1) ~/.claude → /home/vscode/.claude (Claude Code auth, including .claude.json); (2) ~/.config/gh → /home/vscode/.config/gh (gh CLI auth for private repos and pushing); (3) ~/.gitconfig → /tmp/host-gitconfig (git identity, then cp'd to /home/vscode/.gitconfig in postCreateCommand). Without these, a launched BC cannot: clone private repos (shopsystem-docs is private), authenticate to GitHub for push operations, run Claude Code at all, or commit with the operator's identity. bc-launcher today passes only --network shopsystem and -e SHOPMSG_DSN. Fix: bc-container launch must replicate the devcontainer credential-passing pattern, propagating the same three mounts (or an equivalent that achieves the same outcome) and applying any necessary postCreateCommand-equivalent setup steps. Empirical evidence: 'bc-container launch shopsystem-docs --repo-url https://github.com/dstengle/shopsystem-docs.git --shopmsg-dsn ... --network shopsystem' generated docker run with only -e SHOPMSG_DSN and --network shopsystem, missing all credential mounts.
acceptance_criteria: bc-container launch <bc> mounts ~/.claude, ~/.config/gh, and ~/.gitconfig (or their effective equivalents) into the launched container; the launched container can run 'gh auth status' successfully, can run 'claude --version' successfully, and can git push to a private dstengle/* repo successfully.
notes:
PO is authoring Gherkin scenarios in background; pending PO completion before architect dispatch. Will be dispatched as assign_scenarios to shopsystem-bc-launcher. Architect previously announced on-wire work_id will be 'lead-gua' (confabulated; bd doesn't have lead-* prefix in this shop).
Dispatched as assign_scenarios to shopsystem-bc-launcher with on-wire work_id 'shopsystem-templates-918' (architect used bd ID directly, coherent with wire — best approach). 7 scenarios attached with hashes: f51f21bb8219af1b, 636ce0c8a761dead, 58b727750607745f, 93ce008302cf94cb, 85202a4076f45fba, dc9b988512717923, 4ba55450ec351623. Verified valid on wire.
Clarify resolution (PO, 2026-05-22): Behavior (b) — warn-to-stderr-and-proceed — chosen for the missing-.claude.json case. Rationale: brief 007 commits to minimum-friction adoption with agent-consumable signals; (c) silent skip violates the agent-consumable bar; (a) fast-fail is overreach because .claude.json is commonly absent on fresh installs / pre-auth states / version variations (unlike the directory-level missing case, scenario 4ba55450ec351623, which signals a configuration error). New sibling scenario authored: features/launcher-credentials/08-missing-claude-json-warns-and-proceeds.gherkin (Given host ~/.claude exists AND .claude.json does not exist; When launch; Then exits zero, stderr contains '.claude.json' and 'not found', docker run was issued, no .claude.json cp exec_run was issued, tmux new-session exec_run was issued).

CLI/mechanism finding surfaced to Architect: shop-msg respond clarify is BC-side only (BC→lead direction); there is NO lead→BC clarify-response vehicle in the current CLI (shop-msg send subcommands are {assign_scenarios, request_bugfix, request_maintenance}). The collision attempting 'shop-msg respond clarify --bc shopsystem-bc-launcher --work-id shopsystem-templates-918' triggered 'refusing to overwrite existing response' because the BC's own clarify is already the (lead-inbox, work_id, message_type=clarify) row. PO answer must therefore land via Architect dispatch as request_bugfix carrying the new scenario file + decision rationale in --description. PO template guidance on 'respond via shop-msg respond clarify' lags the actual CLI contract; recommend a follow-up brief/PDR or template revision (PDR-001 is already in-flight on template revision). Bead remains IN_PROGRESS pending Architect dispatch.

title: canonical BC primer describes obsolete filesystem inbox model and inotifywait watcher
description: The canonical BC primer template at src/shop_templates/templates/claude/bc.md has two sections that describe the old filesystem-based mailbox model, which is now obsolete since shop-msg moved mailboxes to postgres. (1) The 'BC inbox/outbox protocol' section says inbox/ holds YAML files and outbox/ holds response YAML files — but shop-msg prime reports 'inbox/outbox are in postgres, not on the filesystem.' (2) The 'Session start: arming the inbox watcher via the in-session Monitor' section instructs the router to arm stdbuf -oL inotifywait -m -e create,moved_to inbox/ — but there is no filesystem inbox directory to watch. The correct session-start pattern is shop-msg prime --bc-root . plus bd prime, then poll with shop-msg pending inbox. The Monitor/inotifywait section and the filesystem inbox/outbox description both need to be replaced.
close_reason: Fixed by lead-5r0/lead-vkp: BC primer updated to postgres model with shop-msg watch session-start pattern

title: canonical bc settings.json hook command shop-msg prime missing --bc-root argument
description: The canonical bc.json settings template at src/shop_templates/templates/claude_settings/bc.json sets the SessionStart hook command to 'shop-msg prime' with no --bc-root argument. The shop-msg prime command requires --bc-root (it is a required flag). The hook will silently fail or error on session start in every bootstrapped BC shop. The correct command is 'shop-msg prime --bc-root .' since hooks run from the repo root.
close_reason: Fixed by lead-5hy: canonical bc.json now uses shop-msg prime --bc-root .

title: canonical bc-implementer/bc-reviewer templates missing Claude Code subagent frontmatter
description: The canonical bc-implementer.md and bc-reviewer.md templates in src/shop_templates/templates/ have no YAML frontmatter (name:, description:, tools:). shop-templates update pours these byte-for-byte into .claude/agents/, leaving agent files with no Claude Code subagent identity. The frontmatter is not shop-specific — name: bc-implementer and tools: Read,Edit,Write,Bash,Grep,Glob are the same for every BC shop. This was discovered when shop-templates update correctly stripped the manually-added wrapper from .claude/agents/ per scenario 575c0f31c5e6e8e6.
close_reason: Fixed by lead-sex: frontmatter added to canonical bc-implementer and bc-reviewer templates

title: request_maintenance: shopsystem-bc-launcher — flip BC_IMAGE constant to published bc-base path
description: Architect split-off from the bc-base publish work (shopsystem-templates-4r0 / on-wire lead-xea). Once shopsystem-devcontainer publishes the new bc-base image to its target ghcr path, repos/shopsystem-bc-launcher/src/bc_launcher/controller.py:24 must be updated from BC_IMAGE = 'ghcr.io/shopsystem/bc-base:latest' to whatever path was published. Blocks-on: shopsystem-templates-4r0 (bc-base publish). On-wire work_id will diverge from bd ID per the bd-prefix-divergence trap; architect will use a lead-* placeholder on the wire and note the bd ID in description.
acceptance_criteria: controller.py:24 BC_IMAGE matches the ghcr path shopsystem-devcontainer published in shopsystem-templates-4r0; 'docker pull' against the new path succeeds from this devcontainer.

title: request_maintenance: shopsystem-devcontainer — add gh CLI to Dockerfile and publish as bc-base image
description: bc-launcher hardcodes BC_IMAGE = ghcr.io/shopsystem/bc-base:latest (controller.py:24) but no such image exists in ghcr.io and no Dockerfile defines it anywhere in repos/. shopsystem-devcontainer's .devcontainer/Dockerfile installs ~95% of what bc-base needs (Python 3.14, uv, git, tmux, Claude Code, shop-msg, shop-templates, shop-test-harness, bd, specstory). Only gh CLI is missing — required for BCs to fetch private repos like shopsystem-docs and to push their own commits. Fix: (a) add gh CLI install to the Dockerfile; (b) publish the resulting image to ghcr.io under the appropriate org convention (current devcontainer publishes under dstengle/* per ghcr.io/dstengle/devcontainer-python-node-claude:latest; the shopsystem/* org bc-launcher hardcodes does not exist — name + org need a decision by the architect at dispatch composition); (c) ensure the published image tag is what bc-launcher's BC_IMAGE constant resolves to (may require a separate update to bc-launcher constant).
design: The Dockerfile already lives in shopsystem-devcontainer/.devcontainer/Dockerfile and is structurally the right shape. The publishing step (who pushes, what tag, what org) is the open architect question. The dstengle org convention is the in-evidence default.
acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile installs gh CLI verified on PATH; image is built and published to a ghcr.io path; that path is reachable via 'docker pull' from the devcontainer; bc-launcher's BC_IMAGE constant either already matches the published path or has a paired beadto update it (architect's call).
notes: Dispatched as request_maintenance to shopsystem-devcontainer with on-wire work_id 'lead-xea'. Verified on wire: shop-msg pending inbox --bc shopsystem-devcontainer shows 'lead-xea request_maintenance'.

title: request_maintenance: shopsystem-devcontainer — re-pour bc-primer.md to fix stale --bc-root flags
description: The poured bc-primer.md in shopsystem-devcontainer (.claude/canonical/bc-primer.md) carries 4 stale references to the removed --bc-root flag on shop-msg subcommands (lines 38, 40, 74, 77 per current diff). The canonical template at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude/bc.md is already on name-based addressing (--bc <name>) per PDR-007. Five other BCs (shopsystem-bc-launcher, shopsystem-messaging, shopsystem-templates, shopsystem-test-harness, shopsystem-docs) show zero stale refs, so the canonical pour mechanism works; shopsystem-devcontainer was apparently bootstrapped before the canonical was updated and has not re-poured since. Fix: re-pour bc-primer.md in shopsystem-devcontainer from the current canonical.
acceptance_criteria: repos/shopsystem-devcontainer/.claude/canonical/bc-primer.md byte-identical to repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude/bc.md (excluding any SHOP_NAME substitution); grep -c -E 'bc-root|lead-root' on the poured file returns 0.
notes: Dispatched as request_maintenance to shopsystem-devcontainer with on-wire work_id 'lead-qfc' (architect confabulated the lead-* ID per canonical convention; bd auto-assigns shopsystem-templates-* prefix in this shop, so the wire ID and bd ID diverge). Verified on wire: shop-msg pending inbox --bc shopsystem-devcontainer shows 'lead-qfc request_maintenance'.
close_reason: Duplicate. Created in error due to router CWD drift (was in templates BC, thought I was in lead repo). The real bead is lead-qfc in the lead repo's bd.

title: Brief: single-container bootstrap orchestrator (sibling to brief 007)
description: Stakeholder commitment (session 2026-05-22 17-30Z): 'Given this system is meant to run entirely in containers, there should be a way to launch the lead container, bootstrap automatically from inside and run the rest of the system. This could start by using docker outside of docker. In short, the work the user needs to do should be minimal.' PO's empirical pre-state finding in brief 007: no such single-container affordance exists today. The substrate (docker-out-of-docker, postgres compose, bc-container launch, shop-templates bootstrap, gh repo create) exists as separate primitives. The user has elected to split scope: brief 007 covers documentation against the current primitives with agent-consumable step layering; brief 008 (this bead) covers the orchestrator capability that lets an adopter launch one container and have the rest stand up. Authoring this brief is solely PO scope: capture stakeholder intent for the minimum-friction behavior, behavioral commitments, the 'orchestrator done' observable end state, the explicit ask boundary (what the orchestrator should AND should NOT do for v1 of this capability), and downstream BC decomposition surfaced as an open question for architect resolution (likely involves shopsystem-templates and/or shopsystem-bc-launcher; may motivate a new BC). Empirical pre-state on adjacent capabilities (gh CLI, docker-out-of-docker, current bootstrap CLI surface) should be cited in the brief to ground the scope.
acceptance_criteria: Brief file lands in briefs/ that pairs with brief 007. It surfaces: stakeholder intent (verbatim), the orchestrator capability's behavioral commitments, the 'orchestrator done' end state, what the orchestrator must NOT do in v1, empirical pre-state on adjacent primitives, and the BC-decomposition question as an open architect decision.

title: Brief: end-user adoption docs in shopsystem-docs (minimum-friction bootstrap, agent-consumable)
description: User wants outward-facing end-user adoption documentation for a downstream adopter to create their own product using the shopsystem framework. Stakeholder decisions (session 2026-05-22): (a) Doc HOME — new repo, shopsystem-docs (or similar canonical name). NOT in shopsystem-product, NOT in shopsystem-templates. (b) Surface — creating GitHub repos / org setup; devcontainer / container launch; (c) Bootstrap UX commitment — system is meant to run entirely in containers; user does MINIMUM work; launch lead container, bootstrap automatically from inside, system stands itself up. Docker-out-of-Docker is one path. (d) Agent-consumable steps — if necessary, the same steps can be documented in a form an agent consumes to execute setup on the user's behalf. lead-po authors the brief that resolves the open scope questions: target persona (human-only adopter? agent-assisted? both?), what counts as 'done' for the bootstrap (lead-shop running and reactive? lead+BCs operational? something else?), what new tooling needs to exist vs. what already exists, and what the downstream BC dispatch shape looks like (likely involves shopsystem-templates for bootstrap automation extensions, plus the new shopsystem-docs repo itself as either a new BC or a sibling).
design: New repo shopsystem-docs is itself a topology decision that may be architect territory (BC decomposition). The brief should surface this as an open question, not pre-decide it. PO authors intent; architect decides BC decomposition after the brief is in.
acceptance_criteria: A brief file lands in briefs/ that resolves: target persona for the doc; what 'bootstrap done' means as an observable end state; empirical pre-state on the container-launching-container path (does it work today via shop-templates bootstrap or similar?); the relationship of shopsystem-docs to existing BCs (is shopsystem-docs itself a BC, a sibling repo, something else?); downstream dispatch shape (which BC(s) own what work, what message types).
notes: Dispatched as assign_scenarios to shopsystem-docs with on-wire work_id 'lead-km9' (earlier this session). Verified on wire: shop-msg pending inbox --bc shopsystem-docs shows 'lead-km9 assign_scenarios'.

title: Brief: new-product bootstrap documentation (lead shop + initial BCs)
description: User ask from session 2026-05-22_16-48-12Z: 'We currently have documentation in INSTALL.md for bootstrapping a BC, but we really need documentation on bootstrapping a new product with a new lead and BCs.' Dispatch was blocked last session by the lead-po/lead-architect subagent frontmatter bug (lead-m0a, since closed). lead-po now drafts the brief that resolves three open scope questions: (1) Doc location — extend INSTALL.md, split into BOOTSTRAP-PRODUCT.md + BOOTSTRAP-BC.md, or push canonical into shopsystem-templates with INSTALL.md as a thin pointer? (2) Scope — lead shop scaffold + beads only, or end-to-end through standing up the four initial BCs (templates, messaging, scenarios, test-harness)? (3) Empirical pre-state — does shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type lead already work or is the lead side undocumented because nobody's exercised it since shopsystem product itself was stood up? Answer (3) drives whether downstream dispatch is request_bugfix (capability exists, doc-pin missing) or assign_scenarios (capability needs to be built/extended).
acceptance_criteria: A brief file lands in briefs/ enumerating: target audience, scope decision (questions 1+2 resolved), empirical pre-state on shop-templates bootstrap --shop-type lead (question 3 resolved by running it), and the downstream dispatch shape (which BC, which message type) that the architect will need to send next.
close_reason: Scope misread by router when dispatching. Brief 007 was authored for internal-facing replicating-shopsystem-product topology. User clarified the real ask is outward-facing end-user adoption docs in a new shopsystem-docs repo, with minimum-friction bootstrap (potentially container-launching-container and agent-driven). Superseded by new bead.

title: request_maintenance: shopsystem-templates — codify router standing rule + session-start drain for pending BC responses
description: Pile-up of unprocessed work_done responses in the lead shop's outbox is a recurring failure mode. Today four work_done responses accumulated across two sessions before the router dispatched the architect to process them. The canonical lead-shop primer (repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude/lead.md) currently documents arming shop-msg watch via the in-session Monitor but does not codify the router's standing reaction to Monitor events, nor a session-start drain step. Codify two rules in canonical claude/lead.md: (A) Standing router rule — on each Monitor event of form '<work_id> work_done', the router dispatches lead-architect to process that work_id; on '<work_id> clarify', the router dispatches lead-po or lead-architect per clarify type (scope/vocabulary → po, architecture → architect). (B) Session-start drain — after arming Monitor, before accepting user work, the router checks shop-msg pending outbox --lead <name> (and shop-msg pending inbox --lead <name> for any clarify rows), and dispatches lead-architect to drain any pre-existing pending responses. Both rules are process/protocol only, no infra change.
design: Process-only change living in the canonical primer (single source of truth). No new tools, no hooks (canonical primer already warns hooks block startup). Pairs with shopsystem-templates-s05 (outbox-inspection commands) — both are doc additions to the same file; architect may bundle into one request_maintenance dispatch or split.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical claude/lead.md has a section explicitly enumerating router reactions to Monitor events and a session-start drain step preceding user-work acceptance. Re-pour propagates to .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md in shopsystem-product. Future sessions: pending outbox is drained at session start automatically, and new work_done events trigger architect dispatch without user prompting.
notes: Bundled into the request_maintenance dispatch sent under work_id shopsystem-templates-s05 (single dispatch, single file edit). Awaiting BC work_done.
close_reason: Bundled with shopsystem-templates-s05 in single request_maintenance dispatch; same BC work_done covered both. Canonical now codifies router standing rule (work_done → architect; clarify → po/architect per type; mechanism_observation → architect) and session-start drain step before accepting user work.

title: request_maintenance: shopsystem-templates — add outbox-inspection commands to canonical lead claude.md
description: The canonical lead-shop primer template at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude/lead.md documents shop-msg watch --lead <name> but does not document the BC-response inspection commands every lead-shop router needs: shop-msg pending outbox --lead <name> [--bc-name <bc>] and shop-msg read outbox --bc <name> --work-id <id>. As a result, shopsystem-product's project-specific .claude/shop/primer.md grew its own (now-stale) examples using the removed --lead-root/--bc-root flags. PDR-007 removed those flags; the project primer fell out of date. Fix path: extend canonical claude/lead.md with the current name-addressed pending-outbox / read-outbox commands, re-pour into shopsystem-product, then drop the duplicate stanza from .claude/shop/primer.md.
design: Lives in canonical lead.md (single source of truth across all lead shops), not in any project-specific shop/primer.md. Aligns with PDR-007 name-addressing migration.
acceptance_criteria: Canonical claude/lead.md documents pending outbox --lead and read outbox --bc --work-id with current syntax; re-pour updates .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md in shopsystem-product; the stale --lead-root/--bc-root examples in .claude/shop/primer.md can be deleted because the canonical now carries them.
notes: Dispatched as request_maintenance to shopsystem-templates with work_id shopsystem-templates-s05; bundles shopsystem-templates-mvx in the same dispatch description. Awaiting BC work_done.
close_reason: BC work_done reconciled: canonical claude/lead.md gained outbox-inspection commands + standing rule for Monitor events + session-start drain; re-pour into .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md verified byte-identical (diff clean, 140 lines).

title: lead-architect didn't re-enumerate full BC scenario_hash set when issuing lead-mnx correction; missed two more conflicting scenarios in sibling feature file
description:
Second instance of the same mechanism failure pattern that shopsystem-templates-dzb already names. After clarify lead-eyx-clarify named 3 conflicting BC-side scenarios in bootstrap_cli_surface.feature, the architect's lead-mnx correction authorized retirement of exactly those 3 — but did not re-enumerate the BC's full scenario_hash set, so missed two additional conflicting scenarios (cad1153ef4dfd18c, 9f15982aa00829f1) in the SIBLING file brief_003_event_driven_activation_and_settings_json_bootstrap.feature that pin the same artifact (bootstrap-written CLAUDE.md content). Implementer emitted second clarify on lead-mnx.

This refines dzb's proposed action: pre-state empirical enumeration must apply not just on the initial dispatch but on every clarify-driven correction touching the same artifact.

title: lead-architect skipped BC-side feature/ scan during pre-state for lead-eyx; dispatch claimed no BC-side @scenario_hash conflicts when three exist
description:
During dispatch lead-eyx (assign_scenarios for PDR-003 alternative F — four-file CLAUDE.md split), the dispatch text asserted (in scenario e377fe01b6b708fc's scenario-body preamble): "Lead-shop scenario 39 was authored but never landed as a pinned BC-side scenario in repos/shopsystem-templates/features/, so there is no BC-side @scenario_hash to retire — only the lead-shop intent."

That claim is wrong. Three BC-side scenarios in features/bootstrap_cli_surface.feature directly conflict with the new scenarios:

- @scenario_hash:56a0ac7107ba5c15 (line 152) — "update does not modify CLAUDE.md"; contradicted by new 399908ab1dead40a (update DOES overwrite CLAUDE.md).
- @scenario_hash:a15dac2f87549b8a (line 212) — "bootstrap-written CLAUDE.md contains shop name"; contradicted by new 7399c0655ffa3645 (CLAUDE.md is byte-for-byte canonical body, no splicing).
- @scenario_hash:0cce58eb573d3c91 (line 200) — single primer template = source-of-truth for CLAUDE.md body; superseded by new b5994058515b07eb's two-surface split (body template + primer template).

Implementer emitted clarify (lead-eyx-clarify.yaml) rather than proceeding.

The mechanism failure is the architect's pre-state-verification step. The lead-architect role template says: "Reading the BC's code is hypothesis; running it is fact. Construct a concrete input that exhibits (or fails to exhibit) the behavior; observe; cite the demonstration in the dispatch description." For assign_scenarios that ALSO retires/contradicts prior coverage, "the relevant pre-state" includes the BC's existing @scenario_hash set — which is empirically observable via `grep -n "@scenario_hash:" repos/<bc>/features/*.feature`, not by reading lead-side scenario-register notes alone. The dispatch's misstatement is consistent with the architect having checked only the lead-side register, never the BC-side.

Filing this finding (BC-local) ahead of also emitting a mechanism_observation outbox so the lead's reconciliation sees it.

title: Templates can't route via shop-msg alone — no read-inbox, no pending enumeration
description:
The bc-implementer / bc-reviewer / BC-router templates currently instruct direct inspection of inbox/ and outbox/ directories (e.g. 'ls inbox/ outbox/', 'no outbox file for work_id means unprocessed', 'read inbox/<work_id>.yaml'). This leaks shop-msg's internal storage layout into the templates: any change to where or how shop-msg persists messages breaks every template and subagent that consumes them.

shop-msg's read-side surface is incomplete relative to what the templates assume:
  - shop-msg read supports only 'outbox'; there is no 'shop-msg read inbox <work_id>'.
  - there is no enumeration command (e.g. 'shop-msg pending', 'shop-msg next', 'shop-msg list --bc <name>') that answers 'is there an unprocessed message, and what is its message_type?'.

This surfaced during processing of lead-kq0 (assign_scenarios) — the routing step required ls'ing inbox/ and outbox/ and grep'ing the inbox YAML for message_type, because no CLI alternative exists.

Paired change spanning two BCs:
  - shopsystem-messaging: grow shop-msg with 'read inbox <work_id>' and a pending/enumeration command sufficient for a router to discover unprocessed work without filesystem inspection.
  - shopsystem-templates: once the capability lands, rewrite the bc-implementer, bc-reviewer, and BC-router CLAUDE.md surfaces (and their canonical sources in src/shop_templates/templates/) to use only the shop-msg CLI — no direct inbox/ or outbox/ references.

This is surfaced from shopsystem-templates because the leak is visible in this BC's product (the templates). The driving gap is in shopsystem-messaging.

title: Bootstrap BC subagents (bc-implementer, bc-reviewer) + router CLAUDE.md
description: This BC repo's .claude/ currently has only settings.json — no bc-implementer or bc-reviewer subagents are wired in, so the main agent has no way to dispatch the BC implementer/reviewer roles when an inbox message arrives. Mirror the parent lead shop's PDR-002 path (a) pattern: inline copies of the canonical templates from src/shop_templates/templates/{bc-implementer,bc-reviewer}.md into .claude/agents/, with subagent frontmatter (name/description/tools) and a subagent operating-context preamble. Update CLAUDE.md so the main agent acts as a BC router that dispatches to those subagents when inbox/ holds an unprocessed message. After this, the lead-kq0 assign_scenarios message currently sitting in inbox/ can be picked up by the bc-implementer subagent.
close_reason: Wired .claude/agents/{bc-implementer,bc-reviewer}.md as inline copies of canonical templates in src/shop_templates/templates/, with subagent frontmatter and operating-context preamble. Updated CLAUDE.md to make the main agent a BC router that dispatches to those subagents on unprocessed inbox messages. Verified verbatim sections match canonical templates byte-for-byte; 45-test BDD+unit suite passes when venv is on PATH. The lead-kq0 assign_scenarios message in inbox/ is now ready to be picked up by bc-implementer on the next dispatch.

title: feature: per-template required section pins (4 scenarios + 1 empirical pre-state)
description: Five scenarios from lead-7zg covering required structural sections in each role template plus the Finding 17/S16 empirical-pre-state pin.

title: feature: CLI surface (list/show happy/show unknown)
description: Three scenarios from lead-7zg covering shop-templates list, show, and show unknown.

value: bd update <id> --comment is NOT a valid flag — silently prints help and exits without erroring obviously. Use --append-notes to add a comment-shaped update. Hit during lead-km9 dispatch (2026-05-22, architect turn). Affects bead lifecycle tracking discipline.

Confirmed empirically 2026-05-22: bd in shopsystem-product lead shop auto-assigns prefix 'shopsystem-templates-*' to new issues — NOT 'lead-*'. Sub-agents repeatedly confabulate lead-* IDs that don't exist (e.g. lead-qfc, lead-xea, lead-km9, lead-n78, lead-gua were all hallucinated as 'real bd issues' by lead-architect in this session). The on-wire work_id can use either form — most coherent approach is to use the actual bd ID (shopsystem-templates-918 style) as work_id directly. When sub-agents claim to have 'claimed bead lead-X', verify with bd show before trusting; the bead probably doesn't exist.

Do not edit lead-shop primer files (.claude/shop/primer.md, .claude/canonical/lead-primer.md, or canonical templates at repos/shopsystem-templates/src/shop_templates/templates/claude/lead.md and lead-{po,architect}.md) directly in this repo. Even shop-specific primer content (e.g. shop-msg CLI examples) routes through shopsystem-templates BC first via request_maintenance / request_bugfix / assign_scenarios; change propagates back via re-pour. Reason: keeps canonical as single source of truth, enforces dispatch discipline, avoids drift across lead shops. How to apply: when an edit is needed in any .claude/ path or in the templates source tree, file a bead and dispatch lead-architect to send the change to shopsystem-templates. Caveats: routine local-state files (bd metadata, .specstory history, devcontainer.json) are still fine to touch directly.

In this lead shop (shopsystem-product), bd auto-assigns the prefix 'shopsystem-templates-' to every issue created via 'bd create' — NOT 'lead-' as the canonical lead-primer assumes. The shop-msg send dispatcher requires work_ids; the architect convention (per lead-primer) is to use lead-* IDs as work_ids. Result: architects confabulate lead-* work_ids that don't exist in bd (e.g. lead-qfc dispatched to shopsystem-devcontainer corresponds to bd issue shopsystem-templates-d67). Workaround until bd is reconfigured: (1) use the bd-assigned ID directly as the work_id on the wire (passes a longer string but stays coherent); OR (2) maintain explicit phantom-work_id mappings in each bead's notes (current approach). Root fix: bd config should assign 'lead-' prefix in this shop. Filed as bead for follow-up.
